title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host process ID namespace

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host process ID namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostPID flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's PID namespace can inspect processes running outside the container. If the container also has access to ptrace capabilities this can be used to escalate privileges outside of the container. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers to share the host PID namespace. If you need to run containers which require hostPID, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with spec.hostPID: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that each policy disallows the admission of hostPID containers
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of hostPID containers. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of hostPID containers. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostPID: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_hostpid_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all namespaces in the cluster using the Kubernetes API (e.g., `kubectl get ns`).
- For each namespace, check for the presence and configuration of Pod Security Admission (PSA) labels or PodSecurityPolicies (if still in use), or any other admission controller policies (e.g., OPA/Gatekeeper, Kyverno) that restrict the use of `spec.hostPID: true` in pods.
    - For PSA: Inspect the `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce` (and optionally `audit` and `warn`) labels. Ensure the level is set to `baseline` or `restricted`, both of which disallow `hostPID: true` by default.
    - For PodSecurityPolicy: List all PSPs (`kubectl get psp -o yaml`) and check that policies with `hostPID: true` are not permitted, or are only bound to tightly scoped service accounts/roles.
    - For OPA/Gatekeeper/Kyverno: List all ConstraintTemplates/Constraints or ClusterPolicies/Policies and verify that there is a policy denying pods with `spec.hostPID: true`.
- For each namespace, confirm that there is at least one policy in effect that denies pods with `spec.hostPID: true`, unless explicitly allowed for tightly controlled service accounts.
- Pass Criteria: Every namespace with user workloads has a Pod Security Admission level of at least `baseline` or `restricted`, or an equivalent policy (PSP, OPA, Kyverno) that denies `hostPID: true` except for explicitly allowed cases.
- Example kubectl commands:
    - `kubectl get ns --show-labels`
    - `kubectl get psp -o yaml`
    - `kubectl get constraints` (for OPA/Gatekeeper)
    - `kubectl get cpol,pol -A -o yaml` (for Kyverno)
- Fail if any namespace allows pods with `spec.hostPID: true` without explicit, tightly scoped exception.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.8.0 PDF
